Title: Stereochemical insights into sarpagan and akuammiline alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtaAAR was identified from Amsonia tabernaemontana as a new akuammidine aldehyde reductase. 2. Recombinant AtaAAR reduced SBE-derived sarpagan aldehydes predominantly to akuammidine with minor polyneuridine, explaining akuammidine formation. 3. AtaAAR also reduced vellosimine epimers to tombozine epimers, indicating broader downstream tailoring activity with C16 epimerization outcomes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from A. tabernaemontana leaf transcriptome.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro reduction assays with SBE products or polyneuridine aldehyde; leaf protein assays from A. tabernaemontana.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of akuammidine as the major reduced sarpagan alkaloid product from sarpagan aldehydes; also forms tombozine epimers from vellosimine epimers.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: A. tabernaemontana leaf RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA1256117.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping to AtaAAR not specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with significant pharmaceutical potential.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aldehyde reductase; homologous to CrRedox2 and AsRHR1/2
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway engineering of sarpagan/akuammiline alkaloid stereochemistry toward akuammidine- and tombozine-type products.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21DE3.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reduction likely involves sarpagan bridge opening and C16 epimerization before or during aldehyde reduction. Pure polyneuridine aldehyde was reduced to roughly equal alcohol epimers by recombinant AtaAAR, whereas leaf proteins favored akuammidine more strongly.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome candidate search; recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay with SBE products and polyneuridine aldehyde; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified RsPNAE decarbomethoxylated polyneuridine aldehyde and SBE products to produce both vellosimine C16 epimers. 2. Early products were enriched for 16-epi-vellosimine, which gradually epimerized to vellosimine upon longer incubation. 3. This activity helps explain downstream formation of tombozine/sarpagine-type stereochemistry after subsequent reduction.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short vs prolonged in vitro incubation strongly affected vellosimine epimer ratio.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts sarpagan aldehydes into vellosimine epimers and promotes time-dependent epimerization toward vellosimine.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with significant pharmaceutical potential.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering decarbomethoxylated sarpagan branch products and controlling flux toward vellosimine/tombozine/sarpagine-type alkaloids.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21DE3.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PNAE-mediated epimerization occurs over time, shifting the product pool from 16-epi-vellosimine to vellosimine.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein purification; time-course in vitro enzyme assay with pure polyneuridine aldehyde and SBE products; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrSBE was identified as an epidermis-specific GO homolog in Catharanthus roseus and validated in planta as necessary for sarpagan alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. VIGS of CrSBE reduced pericyclivine and, in CrGO-co-silenced plants, strongly reduced pericyclivine and perivine while geissoschizine accumulated. 3. Recombinant CrSBE cyclized geissoschizine to a sarpagan aldehyde product pool consistent mainly with polyneuridine aldehyde and minor akuammidine aldehyde contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf epidermis-specific transcript; EST counts detected in epidermis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. roseus leaf epidermal transcriptome (Murata et al., 2008).
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced sarpagan alkaloids but did not affect major leaf alkaloids when alone; in CrGO co-silencing background major MIAs were also reduced due to upstream flux perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control enzyme competing with GO for geissoschizine substrate in sarpagan vs other MIA branch selection.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: polyneuridine aldehyde (predominant inferred); minor akuammidine aldehyde possible
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of sarpagan alkaloids pericyclivine and perivine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS in planta; qRT-PCR; heterologous yeast expression; microsome enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; NMR-supported product/stereochemistry analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in 4-wk-old greenhouse-grown plants; co-silencing with CrGO redirects flux and reveals physiological role.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrGO (substrate competitor); downstream deformylase activity in C. roseus leaf proteins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially leaf epidermis context for pathway formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to form the sarpagan bridge, yielding primarily polyneuridine aldehyde-type product.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to reroute flux toward 16S-sarpagan MIAs in metabolic engineering, especially combined with manipulation of competing CrGO and downstream tailoring enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; sarpagan branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for biosynthesis of 16S-sarpagan alkaloids pericyclivine and perivine in C. roseus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer SBE likely yields mainly 16R polyneuridine aldehyde, though CrSBE may produce some akuammidine aldehyde ab initio; downstream enzymatic epimerization explains natural 16S products.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch-point cyclization step after geissoschizine formation

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TeSBE was newly characterized from Tabernaemontana elegans. 2. Recombinant TeSBE converted geissoschizine to sarpagan aldehyde product(s) co-eluting with polyneuridine aldehyde. 3. Downstream T. elegans leaf proteins deformylated SBE products and polyneuridine aldehyde to pericyclivine, explaining formation of 16S-sarpagan derivatives such as vobasine in this species.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch enzyme for sarpagan scaffold formation; not directly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: polyneuridine aldehyde (predominant inferred); minor akuammidine aldehyde possible
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of sarpagan alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; downstream leaf protein conversion assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro microsome assays; downstream treatment with T. elegans leaf proteins or NaOH affected stereochemical outcome.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Downstream deformylase activity in T. elegans leaf proteins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to form sarpagan bridge aldehyde products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with downstream deformylase-type activities to produce 16S sarpagan alkaloids in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; sarpagan branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates sarpagan aldehyde precursor pool for pericyclivine/vobasine-type alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TeSBE reduction products showed relatively more akuammidine than VmSBE and RsSBE2 after NaBH4 reduction, suggesting possible minor direct stereochemical divergence or chemical epimerization sensitivity.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmSBE was newly characterized from Vinca minor. 2. Recombinant VmSBE converted geissoschizine to sarpagan aldehyde product(s) co-eluting with polyneuridine aldehyde. 3. Its products supported downstream stereochemical analyses of SBE function across species.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; branch-forming cyclase/oxidase for sarpagan scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of sarpagan alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; chemical reduction/deformylation analyses.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaBH4 reduction and NaOH deformylation used to probe product stereochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to sarpagan bridge aldehyde products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstruction of sarpagan branch chemistry in heterologous hosts and comparative stereochemistry studies.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; sarpagan branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces sarpagan aldehyde precursors from geissoschizine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with CrSBE and TeSBE, NaBH4-reduced products from VmSBE showed lower akuammidine proportion.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Previously known RsSBE2 was included as a comparative SBE. 2. Recombinant RsSBE2 converted geissoschizine to sarpagan aldehyde product(s) co-eluting with polyneuridine aldehyde. 3. RsSBE2 products were used with RsPNAE to demonstrate rapid formation of vellosimine epimers and subsequent epimerization behavior.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of sarpagan alkaloids and vellosimine branch intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; coupled RsPNAE assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reaction time in downstream PNAE assays altered epimer ratios.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to sarpagan bridge aldehyde products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can support engineering of vellosimine/tombozine/sarpagine-related pathways with appropriate downstream enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; sarpagan branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces sarpagan aldehyde precursor pool for vellosimine-related downstream products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NaBH4 reduction products from RsSBE2 contained less akuammidine than CrSBE and TeSBE reactions.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsRHS from Alstonia scholaris was used as a reference rhazimal synthase. 2. Recombinant AsRHS oxidized geissoschizine to rhazimal and akuammicine, with no evidence for rhazinaline. 3. Combined with reduction and deformylation assays, the study confirmed strict 16R stereochemical outcome for RHS products.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-forming cyclization enzyme for akuammiline/rhazimal branch; not directly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: rhazimal; preakuammicine aldehyde iminium leading to akuammicine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms rhazimal and also forms precursor to akuammicine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; NaBH4 reduction; comparison with authentic standards; plant leaf protein conversion assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with microsomes; A. scholaris leaf proteins further converted rhazimal to strictamine and rhazimol.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Downstream reductase/deformylase activities in A. scholaris leaf proteins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine through C7-C16 bond formation to produce rhazimal (16R) and preakuammicine aldehyde iminium that decomposes to akuammicine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for constructing akuammiline/rhazimal branch in heterologous hosts; should be paired with species-specific deformylase or reductase for strictamine or rhazimol production.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; akuammiline/rhazimal branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces strictly 16R rhazimal and akuammicine from geissoschizine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RHSs and GOs are phylogenetically closer than SBEs and share stereospecificity related to preakuammicine aldehyde iminium formation.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtaRHS was newly identified from Amsonia tabernaemontana. 2. Recombinant AtaRHS oxidized geissoschizine to rhazimal and akuammicine without detectable rhazinaline. 3. The enzyme supports akuammiline biosynthesis in A. tabernaemontana, where rhazimal accumulates in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from transcriptome of A. tabernaemontana.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. tabernaemontana leaf RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA1256117.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-forming cyclization enzyme; not directly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: rhazimal; preakuammicine aldehyde iminium leading to akuammicine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms rhazimal and precursor to akuammicine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome discovery; yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; comparison to authentic rhazimal standard purified from A. tabernaemontana.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro microsome assays; A. tabernaemontana leaf proteins lacked rhazimal deformylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to rhazimal (16R) and preakuammicine aldehyde iminium/akuammicine branch product.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to engineer rhazimal/akuammicine production; absence of endogenous deformylase in source species suggests need for additional tailoring enzymes to obtain strictamine.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; akuammiline/rhazimal branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces strictly 16R rhazimal and akuammicine from geissoschizine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite phylogenetic proximity to Rhazya stricta, no rhazinaline production was detected.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmRHS was newly identified from Vinca minor. 2. Recombinant VmRHS oxidized geissoschizine to rhazimal and akuammicine, with no evidence of rhazinaline production. 3. This activity, combined with V. minor leaf deformylase activity, explains strictamine biosynthesis in V. minor.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-forming cyclization enzyme; not directly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: rhazimal; preakuammicine aldehyde iminium leading to akuammicine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms rhazimal and precursor to akuammicine; rhazimal is further deformylated to strictamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome discovery; yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS; linkage to authentic strictamine and rhazimal chemistry by NMR/leaf protein assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. minor leaf proteins deformylated rhazimal to strictamine.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Downstream rhazimal deformylase activity in V. minor leaf proteins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes 19E-geissoschizine to rhazimal (16R) and preakuammicine aldehyde iminium/akuammicine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive plant-derived monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with pharmaceutical potential.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: One of GenBank accession nos. PV582050 and PQ564467-564471; exact mapping not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering strictamine-producing systems when paired with V. minor-type rhazimal deformylase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis; akuammiline/rhazimal branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces strictly 16R rhazimal and akuammicine precursors supporting strictamine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Provides biochemical explanation for strictamine accumulation in V. minor.
